etymology.description: semantic 广(广) (guǎng, 'shelter') + phonetic 夏(夏) (xià, 'summer') -> large building offering shelter
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 广(广) (guǎng, 'shelter') suggests a structure or building, while the phonetic component 夏(夏) (xià) contributes its sound. Combining these, the character represents a large building capable of housing many people, akin to a mansion.
etymology.simplification: Simplified to 厦 by removing the shared top radical 广(广) (guǎng, 'shelter') in traditional form 廈.

examples.chinese: 这座豪华的厦(這座豪華的廈)吸引了很多游客。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zuò háohuá de xià xīyǐnle hěn duō yóukè.
examples.english: This luxurious mansion attracts many tourists.

examples.chinese: 他住在郊区的一座大厦(他住在郊區的一座大廈)，风景很美(風景很美)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zhù zài jiāoqū de yī zuò dàxià, fēngjǐng hěn měi.
examples.english: He lives in a mansion in the suburbs, and the view is beautiful.

examples.chinese: 这栋老厦(這棟老廈)拥有悠久的历史。
examples.pinyin: Zhè dòng lǎo xià yǒngyǒu yōujiǔ de lìshǐ.
examples.english: This old mansion has a long history.
